verse_specific_anchors: atha cet — if-then conditional opening; preserved as the conditional structure of the engineering layer
imam — this; the verse's scoping word that makes the claim about a specific case, not all cases
dharmyam saṅgrāmam — this righteous engagement; mapped to the genuinely role-aligned engineering duty
sva-dharmaṁ kīrtiṁ ca hitvā — abandoning duty and reputation; the loss-pair preserved in the analog
pāpam avāpsyasi — incurring sin; mapped to organisational debt
verdict_reason: The engineering analog anchors specifically on the if-then form (the 'if you walk away from this' structure) and the imam-scoped specificity. The day-six-of-an-active-incident scenario is uniquely the 2.33 analog: the duty is already in motion, the operator's specific WAL-plugin context is the un-pickup-able element. Other karma-yoga verses cannot be force-fit: 2.31 names the role-bound duty as such; 2.32 names the unsought arrival; 2.34 names the imperishable disrepute consequence; 2.35 names the peer-witness consequence. Only 2.33 names the if-then form: 'IF you do not perform this, THEN three losses follow.' The conditional structure is irreducibly 2.33. PASS on both 7.6 and 3.5.
